1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم حم
1.english: Ha, Meem.
1.transliteration: Haa Meeem

1.arabic: تنزيل من الرحمن الرحيم
1.english: [This is] a revelation from the Entirely Merciful, the Especially Merciful -
1.transliteration: Tanzeelum Minar-Rahmaanir-Raheem

1.arabic: كتاب فصلت آياته قرآنا عربيا لقوم يعلمون
1.english: A Book whose verses have been detailed, an Arabic Qur'an for a people who know,
1.transliteration: Kitaabun fussilat Aayaatuhoo Qur-aanan 'Arabiyyal liqawminy ya'lamoon

1.arabic: بشيرا ونذيرا فأعرض أكثرهم فهم لا يسمعون
1.english: As a giver of good tidings and a warner; but most of them turn away, so they do not hear.
1.transliteration: Basheeranw wa nazeeran fa-a'rada aksaruhum fahum laa yasma'oon

1.arabic: وقالوا قلوبنا في أكنة مما تدعونا إليه وفي آذاننا وقر ومن بيننا وبينك حجاب فاعمل إننا عاملون
1.english: And they say, "Our hearts are within coverings from that to which you invite us, and in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a partition, so work; indeed, we are working."
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo quloobunaa feee akinnatim mimmaa tad'oonaaa ilaihi wa feee aazaaninaa waqrunw wa mim baininaa wa bainika bijaabun fa'mal innanaa 'aamiloon

1.arabic: قل إنما أنا بشر مثلكم يوحى إلي أنما إلهكم إله واحد فاستقيموا إليه واستغفروه ۗ وويل للمشركين
1.english: Say, O [Muhammad], "I am only a man like you to whom it has been revealed that your god is but one God; so take a straight course to Him and seek His forgiveness." And woe to those who associate others with Allah -
1.transliteration: Qul innamaaa ana basharum mislukum yoohaaa ilaiya annamaaa ilaahukum Ilaahunw Waahidun fastaqeemooo ilaihi wastagfirooh; wa wailul lil mushrikeen

1.arabic: الذين لا يؤتون الزكاة وهم بالآخرة هم كافرون
1.english: Those who do not give zakah, and in the Hereafter they are disbelievers.
1.transliteration: Allazeena laa yu'toonaz Zakaata wa hum bil-Aakhiratihum kaafiroon

1.arabic: إن الذين آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات لهم أجر غير ممنون
1.english: Indeed, those who believe and do righteous deeds - for them is a reward uninterrupted.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena aamanoo wa 'amilus saalihaati lahum ajrun ghairu mamnoon

1.arabic: ۞ قل أئنكم لتكفرون بالذي خلق الأرض في يومين وتجعلون له أندادا ۚ ذلك رب العالمين
1.english: Say, "Do you indeed disbelieve in He who created the earth in two days and attribute to Him equals? That is the Lord of the worlds."
1.transliteration: Qul a'innakum latakfuroona billazee khalaqal arda fee yawmaini wa taj'aloona lahooo andaadaa; zaalika Rabbul 'aalameen

1.arabic: وجعل فيها رواسي من فوقها وبارك فيها وقدر فيها أقواتها في أربعة أيام سواء للسائلين
1.english: And He placed on the earth firmly set mountains over its surface, and He blessed it and determined therein its [creatures'] sustenance in four days without distinction - for [the information] of those who ask.
1.transliteration: Wa ja'ala feehaa rawaa siya min fawqihaa wa baaraka feehaa wa qaddara feehaaaa aqwaatahaa feee arba'ati ayyaamin sawaaa'al lissaaa'ileen

1.arabic: ثم استوى إلى السماء وهي دخان فقال لها وللأرض ائتيا طوعا أو كرها قالتا أتينا طائعين
1.english: Then He directed Himself to the heaven while it was smoke and said to it and to the earth, "Come [into being], willingly or by compulsion." They said, "We have come willingly."
1.transliteration: Summas tawaaa ilas-samaaa'i wa hiya dukhaanun faqaala lahaa wa lil ardi'tiyaaa taw'an aw karhan qaalataaa atainaa taaa'i'een

1.arabic: فقضاهن سبع سماوات في يومين وأوحى في كل سماء أمرها ۚ وزينا السماء الدنيا بمصابيح وحفظا ۚ ذلك تقدير العزيز العليم
1.english: And He completed them as seven heavens within two days and inspired in each heaven its command. And We adorned the nearest heaven with lamps and as protection. That is the determination of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.
1.transliteration: Faqadaahunna sab'a samaawaatin fee yawmaini wa awhaa fee kulli samaaa'in amarahaa; wa zaiyannassa maaa'ad dunyaa bimasaabeeha wa hifzaa; zaalika taqdeerul 'Azeezil 'Aleem

1.arabic: فإن أعرضوا فقل أنذرتكم صاعقة مثل صاعقة عاد وثمود
1.english: But if they turn away, then say, "I have warned you of a thunderbolt like the thunderbolt [that struck] 'Aad and Thamud.
1.transliteration: Fa-in a'radoo faqul anzartukum saa'iqatam misla saa'iqati 'Aadinw wa Samood

1.arabic: إذ جاءتهم الرسل من بين أيديهم ومن خلفهم ألا تعبدوا إلا الله ۖ قالوا لو شاء ربنا لأنزل ملائكة فإنا بما أرسلتم به كافرون
1.english: [That occurred] when the messengers had come to them before them and after them, [saying], "Worship not except Allah." They said, "If our Lord had willed, He would have sent down the angels, so indeed we, in that with which you have been sent, are disbelievers."
1.transliteration: Iz jaaa'at humur Rusulu mim baini aydeehim wa min khalfihim allaa ta'budooo illal laaha qaaloo law shaaa'a Rabunaa la anzala malaaa 'ikatan fa innaa bimaaa ursiltum bihee kaafiroon

1.arabic: فأما عاد فاستكبروا في الأرض بغير الحق وقالوا من أشد منا قوة ۖ أولم يروا أن الله الذي خلقهم هو أشد منهم قوة ۖ وكانوا بآياتنا يجحدون
1.english: As for 'Aad, they were arrogant upon the earth without right and said, "Who is greater than us in strength?" Did they not consider that Allah who created them was greater than them in strength? But they were rejecting Our signs.
1.transliteration: Fa ammaa 'Aadun fastak baroo fil ardi bighairul haqqi wa qaaloo man ashaddu minnaa quwwatan awalam yaraw annal laahal lazee khalaqahum Huwa ashaddu minhum quwwatanw wa kaanoo bi Aayaatinaa yajhadoon

1.arabic: فأرسلنا عليهم ريحا صرصرا في أيام نحسات لنذيقهم عذاب الخزي في الحياة الدنيا ۖ ولعذاب الآخرة أخزى ۖ وهم لا ينصرون
1.english: So We sent upon them a screaming wind during days of misfortune to make them taste the punishment of disgrace in the worldly life; but the punishment of the Hereafter is more disgracing, and they will not be helped.
1.transliteration: Fa arsalnaa 'alaihim reehan sarsaran feee ayyaamin nahisaatil linuzeeqahum 'azaabal khizyi fil hayaatid dunyaa wa la'azaabul Aakhirati akhzaa wa hum laa yunsaroon

1.arabic: وأما ثمود فهديناهم فاستحبوا العمى على الهدى فأخذتهم صاعقة العذاب الهون بما كانوا يكسبون
1.english: And as for Thamud, We guided them, but they preferred blindness over guidance, so the thunderbolt of humiliating punishment seized them for what they used to earn.
1.transliteration: Wa ammaa Samoodu fahadinaahum fastahabbul 'ama 'alal huda fa akhazathum saa'iqatul 'azaabil hooni bimaa kaanoo yaksiboon

1.arabic: ونجينا الذين آمنوا وكانوا يتقون
1.english: And We saved those who believed and used to fear Allah.
1.transliteration: Wa najjainal lazeena aamanoo wa kaanoo yattaqoon

1.arabic: ويوم يحشر أعداء الله إلى النار فهم يوزعون
1.english: And [mention, O Muhammad], the Day when the enemies of Allah will be gathered to the Fire while they are [driven] assembled in rows,
1.transliteration: Wa yawma yuhsharu a'daaa'ul laahi ilan Naari fahum yooza'oon

1.arabic: حتى إذا ما جاءوها شهد عليهم سمعهم وأبصارهم وجلودهم بما كانوا يعملون
1.english: Until, when they reach it, their hearing and their eyes and their skins will testify against them of what they used to do.
1.transliteration: Hattaaa izaa maa jaaa'oohaa shahida 'alaihim samu'uhum wa absaaruhum wa julooduhum bimaa kaanoo ya'maloon

1.arabic: وقالوا لجلودهم لم شهدتم علينا ۖ قالوا أنطقنا الله الذي أنطق كل شيء وهو خلقكم أول مرة وإليه ترجعون
1.english: And they will say to their skins, "Why have you testified against us?" They will say, "We were made to speak by Allah, who has made everything speak; and He created you the first time, and to Him you are returned.
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo lijuloodihim lima shahittum 'alainaa qaaloo antaqanal laahul lazeee antaqa kulla shai'inw wa Huwa khalaqakum awwala marratinw wa ilaihi turja'oon

1.arabic: وما كنتم تستترون أن يشهد عليكم سمعكم ولا أبصاركم ولا جلودكم ولكن ظننتم أن الله لا يعلم كثيرا مما تعملون
1.english: And you were not covering yourselves, lest your hearing testify against you or your sight or your skins, but you assumed that Allah does not know much of what you do.
1.transliteration: Wa maa kuntum tastatiroona ai-yashhada 'alaikum sam'ukum wa laaa absaarukum wa laa juloodukum wa laakin zanantum annal laaha laa ya'lamu kaseeram mimmaa ta'maloon

1.arabic: وذلكم ظنكم الذي ظننتم بربكم أرداكم فأصبحتم من الخاسرين
1.english: And that was your assumption which you assumed about your Lord. It has brought you to ruin, and you have become among the losers."
1.transliteration: Wa zaalikum zannukumul lazee zanantum bi-Rabbikum ardaakum fa asbahtum minal khaasireen

1.arabic: فإن يصبروا فالنار مثوى لهم ۖ وإن يستعتبوا فما هم من المعتبين
1.english: So [even] if they are patient, the Fire is a residence for them; and if they ask to appease [Allah], they will not be of those who are allowed to appease.
1.transliteration: Fa-iny yasbiroo fan Naaru maswal lahum wa iny-yasta'tiboo famaa hum minal mu'tabeen

1.arabic: ۞ وقيضنا لهم قرناء فزينوا لهم ما بين أيديهم وما خلفهم وحق عليهم القول في أمم قد خلت من قبلهم من الجن والإنس ۖ إنهم كانوا خاسرين
1.english: And We appointed for them companions who made attractive to them what was before them and what was behind them [of sin], and the word has come into effect upon them among nations which had passed on before them of jinn and men. Indeed, they [all] were losers.
1.transliteration: Wa qaiyadnaa lahum quranaaa'a fazaiyanoo lahum maa baina aideehim wa maa khalfahum wa haqqa 'alaihimul qawlu feee umamin qad khalat min qablihim minal jinni wal insi innahum kaanoo khaasireen

1.arabic: وقال الذين كفروا لا تسمعوا لهذا القرآن والغوا فيه لعلكم تغلبون
1.english: And those who disbelieve say, "Do not listen to this Qur'an and speak noisily during [the recitation of] it that perhaps you will overcome."
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazeena kafaroo laa tasma'oo lihaazal Quraani walghaw feehi la'allakum taghlihoon

1.arabic: فلنذيقن الذين كفروا عذابا شديدا ولنجزينهم أسوأ الذي كانوا يعملون
1.english: But We will surely cause those who disbelieve to taste a severe punishment, and We will surely recompense them for the worst of what they had been doing.
1.transliteration: Falanuzeeqannal lazeena kafaroo 'azaaban shadeedanw wa lanajziyannahum aswallazee kaanoo ya'maloon

1.arabic: ذلك جزاء أعداء الله النار ۖ لهم فيها دار الخلد ۖ جزاء بما كانوا بآياتنا يجحدون
1.english: That is the recompense of the enemies of Allah - the Fire. For them therein is the home of eternity as recompense for what they, of Our verses, were rejecting.
1.transliteration: Zaalika jazaaa'u a'daaa'il laahin Naaru lahum feehaa daarul khuld, jazaaa'am bimaa kaanoo bi aayaatinaa yajhdoon

1.arabic: وقال الذين كفروا ربنا أرنا اللذين أضلانا من الجن والإنس نجعلهما تحت أقدامنا ليكونا من الأسفلين
1.english: And those who disbelieved will [then] say, "Our Lord, show us those who misled us of the jinn and men [so] we may put them under our feet that they will be among the lowest."
1.transliteration: Wa qaalal lazeena kafaroo Rabbanaaa arinal lazaini adal laanaa minal jinni wal insi naj'alhumaa tahta aqdaaminaa liyakoonaa minal asfaleen

1.arabic: إن الذين قالوا ربنا الله ثم استقاموا تتنزل عليهم الملائكة ألا تخافوا ولا تحزنوا وأبشروا بالجنة التي كنتم توعدون
1.english: Indeed, those who have said, "Our Lord is Allah " and then remained on a right course - the angels will descend upon them, [saying], "Do not fear and do not grieve but receive good tidings of Paradise, which you were promised.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena qaaloo Rabbunal laahu summas taqaamoo tatanazzalu 'alaihimul malaaa 'ikatu allaa takhaafoo wa laa tahzanoo wa abshiroo bil Jannnatil latee kuntum too'adoon

1.arabic: نحن أولياؤكم في الحياة الدنيا وفي الآخرة ۖ ولكم فيها ما تشتهي أنفسكم ولكم فيها ما تدعون
1.english: We [angels] were your allies in worldly life and [are so] in the Hereafter. And you will have therein whatever your souls desire, and you will have therein whatever you request [or wish]
1.transliteration: Nahnu awliyaaa'ukum fil hayaatid dunyaa wa fil Aakhirati wa lakum feehaa maa tashtaheee anfusukum wa lakum feehaa ma tadda'oon

1.arabic: نزلا من غفور رحيم
1.english: As accommodation from a [Lord who is] Forgiving and Merciful."
1.transliteration: Nuzulam min Ghafoorir Raheem

1.arabic: ومن أحسن قولا ممن دعا إلى الله وعمل صالحا وقال إنني من المسلمين
1.english: And who is better in speech than one who invites to Allah and does righteousness and says, "Indeed, I am of the Muslims."
1.transliteration: Wa man ahsanu qawlam mimman da'aaa ilal laahi wa 'amila saalihanw wa qaala innanee minal muslimeen

1.arabic: ولا تستوي الحسنة ولا السيئة ۚ ادفع بالتي هي أحسن فإذا الذي بينك وبينه عداوة كأنه ولي حميم
1.english: And not equal are the good deed and the bad. Repel [evil] by that [deed] which is better; and thereupon the one whom between you and him is enmity [will become] as though he was a devoted friend.
1.transliteration: Wa laa tastawil hasanatu wa las saiyi'ah; idfa' billatee hiya ahsanu fa'izal lazee bainaka wa bainahoo 'adaawatun ka'annahoo waliyun hameem

1.arabic: وما يلقاها إلا الذين صبروا وما يلقاها إلا ذو حظ عظيم
1.english: But none is granted it except those who are patient, and none is granted it except one having a great portion [of good].
1.transliteration: Wa maa yulaqqaahaaa illal lazeena sabaroo wa maa yulaqqaahaaa illaa zoo hazzin 'azeem

1.arabic: وإما ينزغنك من الشيطان نزغ فاستعذ بالله ۖ إنه هو السميع العليم
1.english: And if there comes to you from Satan an evil suggestion, then seek refuge in Allah. Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Knowing.
1.transliteration: Wa immaa yanzaghannaka minash Shaitaani nazghun fasta'iz billaahi innahoo Huwas Samee'ul 'Aleem

1.arabic: ومن آياته الليل والنهار والشمس والقمر ۚ لا تسجدوا للشمس ولا للقمر واسجدوا لله الذي خلقهن إن كنتم إياه تعبدون
1.english: And of His signs are the night and day and the sun and moon. Do not prostrate to the sun or to the moon, but prostate to Allah, who created them, if it should be Him that you worship.
1.transliteration: Wa min Aayaatihil lailu wannahaaru washshamsu walqamar; laa tasjudoo lishshamsi wa laa lilqamari wasjudoo lillaahil lazee khala qahunna in kuntum iyyaahu ta'budoon

1.arabic: فإن استكبروا فالذين عند ربك يسبحون له بالليل والنهار وهم لا يسأمون ۩
1.english: But if they are arrogant - then those who are near your Lord exalt Him by night and by day, and they do not become weary.
1.transliteration: Fa inis-takbaroo fallazee na 'inda Rabbika yusabbihoona lahoo billaili wannnahaari wa hum laa yas'amoon

1.arabic: ومن آياته أنك ترى الأرض خاشعة فإذا أنزلنا عليها الماء اهتزت وربت ۚ إن الذي أحياها لمحيي الموتى ۚ إنه على كل شيء قدير
1.english: And of His signs is that you see the earth stilled, but when We send down upon it rain, it quivers and grows. Indeed, He who has given it life is the Giver of Life to the dead. Indeed, He is over all things competent.
1.transliteration: Wa min Aayaatiheee annaka taral arda khaashi'atan fa izaaa anzalna 'alaihal maaa'ah tazzat wa rabat; innal lazeee ahyaahaa lamuhiyil mawtaa; innahoo 'alaa kulli shai-in Qadeer

1.arabic: إن الذين يلحدون في آياتنا لا يخفون علينا ۗ أفمن يلقى في النار خير أم من يأتي آمنا يوم القيامة ۚ اعملوا ما شئتم ۖ إنه بما تعملون بصير
1.english: Indeed, those who inject deviation into Our verses are not concealed from Us. So, is he who is cast into the Fire better or he who comes secure on the Day of Resurrection? Do whatever you will; indeed, He is Seeing of what you do.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena yulhidoona feee Aayaatina laa yakhfawna 'alainaa' afamai yulqaa fin Naari khayrun am mai yaateee aaminai Yawmal Qiyaamah; i'maloo ma shi'tum innahoo bimaa ta'maloona Baseer

1.arabic: إن الذين كفروا بالذكر لما جاءهم ۖ وإنه لكتاب عزيز
1.english: Indeed, those who disbelieve in the message after it has come to them... And indeed, it is a mighty Book.
1.transliteration: Inna Lazeena kafaroo biz Zikri lammaa jaa'ahum wa innahoo la Kitaabun 'Azeez

1.arabic: لا يأتيه الباطل من بين يديه ولا من خلفه ۖ تنزيل من حكيم حميد
1.english: Falsehood cannot approach it from before it or from behind it; [it is] a revelation from a [Lord who is] Wise and Praiseworthy.
1.transliteration: Laa yaateehil baatilu mim baini yadaihi wa laa min khalfihee tanzeelum min Hakeemin Hameed

1.arabic: ما يقال لك إلا ما قد قيل للرسل من قبلك ۚ إن ربك لذو مغفرة وذو عقاب أليم
1.english: Nothing is said to you, [O Muhammad], except what was already said to the messengers before you. Indeed, your Lord is a possessor of forgiveness and a possessor of painful penalty.
1.transliteration: Maa yuqaalu laka illaa maa qad qeela lir Rusuli min qablik; inna Rabbaka lazoo maghfiratinw wa zoo 'iqaabin aleem

1.arabic: ولو جعلناه قرآنا أعجميا لقالوا لولا فصلت آياته ۖ أأعجمي وعربي ۗ قل هو للذين آمنوا هدى وشفاء ۖ والذين لا يؤمنون في آذانهم وقر وهو عليهم عمى ۚ أولئك ينادون من مكان بعيد
1.english: And if We had made it a non-Arabic Qur'an, they would have said, "Why are its verses not explained in detail [in our language]? Is it a foreign [recitation] and an Arab [messenger]?" Say, "It is, for those who believe, a guidance and cure." And those who do not believe - in their ears is deafness, and it is upon them blindness. Those are being called from a distant place.
1.transliteration: Wa law ja'alnaahu Qur-aanan A'jamiyyal laqaaloo law laa fussilat Aayaatuhoo 'a A'jamiyyunw wa 'Arabiyy; qul huwa lillazeena aamanoo hudanw wa shifaaa'unw wallazeena la yu'minoona feee aazaanihim waqrunw wa huwa 'alaihim 'amaa; ulaaa'ika yunaadawna mim maakaanim ba'eed

1.arabic: ولقد آتينا موسى الكتاب فاختلف فيه ۗ ولولا كلمة سبقت من ربك لقضي بينهم ۚ وإنهم لفي شك منه مريب
1.english: And We had already given Moses the Scripture, but it came under disagreement. And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, it would have been concluded between them. And indeed they are, concerning the Qur'an, in disquieting doubt.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aatainaa Moosal Kitaaba fakhtulifa fee; wa lawlaa Kalimatun sabaqat mir Rabbika laqudiya bainahum; wa innahum lafee shakkim minhu mureeb

1.arabic: من عمل صالحا فلنفسه ۖ ومن أساء فعليها ۗ وما ربك بظلام للعبيد
1.english: Whoever does righteousness - it is for his [own] soul; and whoever does evil [does so] against it. And your Lord is not ever unjust to [His] servants.
1.transliteration: Man 'amila salihan falinafshihee wa man asaaa'a fa'alaihaa; wamaa rabbuka bizallaamil lil 'abeed

1.arabic: ۞ إليه يرد علم الساعة ۚ وما تخرج من ثمرات من أكمامها وما تحمل من أنثى ولا تضع إلا بعلمه ۚ ويوم يناديهم أين شركائي قالوا آذناك ما منا من شهيد
1.english: To him [alone] is attributed knowledge of the Hour. And fruits emerge not from their coverings nor does a female conceive or give birth except with His knowledge. And the Day He will call to them, "Where are My 'partners'?" they will say, "We announce to You that there is [no longer] among us any witness [to that]."
1.transliteration: Ilaihi yuraddu 'ilmus Saaa'ah; wa maa takhruju min samaraatim min akmaamihaa wa maa tahmilu min unsaa wa laa tada'u illaa bi'ilmih; wa Yawma yunaadeehim aina shurakaaa'ee qaalooo aazannaaka maa minnaa min shaheed

1.arabic: وضل عنهم ما كانوا يدعون من قبل ۖ وظنوا ما لهم من محيص
1.english: And lost from them will be those they were invoking before, and they will be certain that they have no place of escape.
1.transliteration: Wa dalla 'anhum maa kaanoo yad'oona min qablu wa zannoo maa lahum mim mahees

1.arabic: لا يسأم الإنسان من دعاء الخير وإن مسه الشر فيئوس قنوط
1.english: Man is not weary of supplication for good [things], but if evil touches him, he is hopeless and despairing.
1.transliteration: Laa yas'amul insaanu min du'aaa'il khairi wa im massa hush sharru fa ya'oosun qanoot

1.arabic: ولئن أذقناه رحمة منا من بعد ضراء مسته ليقولن هذا لي وما أظن الساعة قائمة ولئن رجعت إلى ربي إن لي عنده للحسنى ۚ فلننبئن الذين كفروا بما عملوا ولنذيقنهم من عذاب غليظ
1.english: And if We let him taste mercy from Us after an adversity which has touched him, he will surely say, "This is [due] to me, and I do not think the Hour will occur; and [even] if I should be returned to my Lord, indeed, for me there will be with Him the best." But We will surely inform those who disbelieved about what they did, and We will surely make them taste a massive punishment.
1.transliteration: Wa la in azaqnaahu rahmatam minnaa mim ba'di dar raaa'a massat hu la yaqoolanna haazaa lee wa maaa azunnus Saa'ata qaaa'imatanw wa la'in ruji'tu ilaa Rabbeee inna lee 'indahoo lalhusnaa; falanu nabbi'annal lazeena kafaroo bimaa 'amiloo wa lanuzeeqan nahum min 'azaabin ghaleez

1.arabic: وإذا أنعمنا على الإنسان أعرض ونأى بجانبه وإذا مسه الشر فذو دعاء عريض
1.english: And when We bestow favor upon man, he turns away and distances himself; but when evil touches him, then he is full of extensive supplication.
1.transliteration: Wa izaaa an'amnaa 'alal insaani a'rada wa na-aa bijaani bihee wa izaa massahush sharru fazoo du'aaa'in 'areed

1.arabic: قل أرأيتم إن كان من عند الله ثم كفرتم به من أضل ممن هو في شقاق بعيد
1.english: Say, "Have you considered: if the Qur'an is from Allah and you disbelieved in it, who would be more astray than one who is in extreme dissension?"
1.transliteration: Qul araiaitum in kaana min 'indil laahi summa kafar tum bihee man adallu mimman huwa fee shiqaqim ba'eed

1.arabic: سنريهم آياتنا في الآفاق وفي أنفسهم حتى يتبين لهم أنه الحق ۗ أولم يكف بربك أنه على كل شيء شهيد
1.english: We will show them Our signs in the horizons and within themselves until it becomes clear to them that it is the truth. But is it not sufficient concerning your Lord that He is, over all things, a Witness?
1.transliteration: Sanureehim Aayaatinaa fil aafaaqi wa feee anfusihim hattaa yatabaiyana lahum annahul haqq; awa lam yakfi bi Rabbika annahoo 'alaa kulli shai-in Shaheed

1.arabic: ألا إنهم في مرية من لقاء ربهم ۗ ألا إنه بكل شيء محيط
1.english: Unquestionably, they are in doubt about the meeting with their Lord. Unquestionably He is, of all things, encompassing.
1.transliteration: Alaaa innahum fee miryatim mil liqaaa'i Rabbihim; alaaa innahoo bikulli shai'im muheet
